CBC.description: A test that measures different components of blood

RBC.name: Red Blood Cells
RBC.description: Cells that carry oxygen throughout your body

HGB.name: Hemoglobin
HGB.description: Protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen

HCT.name: Hematocrit
HCT.description: Percentage of blood made up of red blood cells

MCV.name: Mean Corpuscular Volume
MCV.description: Average size of red blood cells

BMP.description: Tests that measure kidney function, blood sugar, and electrolytes

Calcium.name: Calcium
Calcium.description: Mineral important for bones and muscles

Potassium.name: Potassium
Potassium.description: Electrolyte important for heart and muscle function

Creatinine.name: Creatinine
Creatinine.description: Waste product that shows how well kidneys are working

Lipid_Panel.description: Tests that measure cholesterol and fats in your blood

Total_Cholesterol.name: Total Cholesterol
Total_Cholesterol.description: Overall amount of cholesterol in blood

LDL.name: LDL Cholesterol
LDL.description: Bad cholesterol that can clog arteries

HDL.name: HDL Cholesterol
HDL.description: Good cholesterol that helps remove bad cholesterol

Triglycerides.name: Triglycerides
Triglycerides.description: Type of fat in your blood

Thyroid.description: Tests that check how well your thyroid gland is working

Liver.description: Tests that check how well your liver is working

AST.name: Aspartate Aminotransferase
AST.description: Enzyme found in liver and other organs

ALP.name: Alkaline Phosphatase
ALP.description: Enzyme related to liver and bones

Bilirubin.name: Total Bilirubin
Bilirubin.description: Substance produced when red blood cells break down

radiology_common.consolidation: Area where air spaces in lungs are filled with fluid or cells
radiology_common.effusion: Buildup of fluid in a space that normally has little fluid
radiology_common.pneumothorax: Air in the space around the lungs (collapsed lung)
radiology_common.atelectasis: Partial or complete collapse of lung
radiology_common.lucency: Area that allows X-rays through (appears dark)

ct_mri_common.enhancement: Area that shows up brighter after contrast dye
ct_mri_common.infarct: Area of dead tissue due to lack of blood supply
ct_mri_common.stenosis: Narrowing of a passage or vessel

general_terms.negative: Test did not find what it was looking for (often good news)
general_terms.positive: Test found what it was looking for
